title: ee.mesh.hello.v1
description: Tiny bounded handshake request that SRR6.46.2 autodiscovery sends to candidate peers. Pure-read; the responder writes no DB rows. Payload ≤ 4096 bytes (frame budget enforced by the SRR6.9 transport).

requestId.description: Caller-side correlation token. The responder echoes this in `requestId` of `ee.mesh.hello.response.v1` so the caller can match concurrent in-flight probes.

requesterNodeKey.description: Stable tailscale node identifier of the requesting host. Redactable under the `tailscale_metadata` class.

requesterEeVersion.description: ee crate version (e.g. `0.2.0`). Informational; not used for protocol gating.

requesterEeProtocolVersion.description: Semver of the mesh wire protocol the requester speaks. Major-version skew is incompatible; minor-version skew is compatible. SRR6.27 owns the upgrade compatibility contract.

requesterWorkspaceIds.description: Workspace-ids the caller cares about for filtering. SRR6.46.2 uses these to drop peers whose `responderWorkspaceIds` does not intersect the caller's.

requesterCapabilities.description: Stable capability tokens the caller advertises (e.g. `discovery`, `revision_notice`, `sync_once`). Forward-compat: unknown tokens are ignored by the responder.

requesterAdvertisedTags.description: Optional: tailscale ACL tags the requester advertises (e.g. `tag:ee-mesh`). Lets the responder enforce SRR6.46.7 `service_tag` policy without a separate tailnet round-trip.
